test_name: 053. Early gold mining in the Otago region

content.cn_text: 好的，今天我们关注的是19世纪60年代的新西兰，特别是黄金
content.en_text: Well, today we're focusing on New Zealand in the 1860s, and in particular, how the

content.cn_text: 的发现如何改变了这个国家。当时，黄金在北美和澳大利亚创造了巨大的财富，
content.en_text: discovery of gold transformed the country. At that time, gold had created great wealth

content.cn_text: 新西兰自然也希望取得同样的成功。
content.en_text: in North America and Australia, and, naturally, New Zealand was hoping for the same success.

content.cn_text: 人们知道新西兰有黄金。1852年发现了一些，引发了一场短暂的淘金热，
content.en_text: People knew there was gold in New Zealand. Some had been found in 1852 leading to a brief

content.cn_text: 人们从世界各地赶来，看看能否挖到黄金。
content.en_text: gold rush in which people had come from all parts of the world to see if they could dig

content.cn_text: 但现在，新西兰需要有经验的淘金者，那些知道去哪里找东西的人，
content.en_text: up any for themselves. But now, New Zealand needed experienced gold prospectors, people

content.cn_text: 去其他地区寻找。因此，当地政府在19世纪50年代末开始
content.en_text: who knew where to look for the stuff, to find in other areas. So what the local government

content.cn_text: 做的事情是承诺给这些勘探者一份奖赏，如果他们在
content.en_text: started doing in the late 1850s was promising a reward of these prospectors discovered

content.cn_text: 他们省内任何地方发现了黄金。最终响应号召的人是澳大利亚矿工
content.en_text: gold anywhere in their province. The man who eventually answered the call was Australian

content.cn_text: 加布里埃尔·里德，他于1861年5月来到奥塔哥。据说他有一次
content.en_text: minor called Gabrielle Reed, who came to Otago in May 1861. The story goes that he was

content.cn_text: 晚上在帐篷外散步时，看到附近一条河里有黄金像星星一样闪烁。
content.en_text: walking outside his tent at night when he saw gold shining like stars in a nearby river.

content.cn_text: 他的发现很快传开，很快数千人涌向奥塔哥
content.en_text: It didn't take long for word of his fine to spread, and soon thousands were coming to Otago

content.cn_text: 参加淘金热。这些人是谁？嗯，来的男人主要是
content.en_text: to take part in the gold rush. Who were these people? Well, the men that came were predominantly

content.cn_text: 劳工和商人，而且通常很年轻，尽管有些人已婚并把家人留在了
content.en_text: laborers and tradesmen, and often very young, although some were married and left their

content.cn_text: 他们来的国家。但女性也来了，来到那些
content.en_text: families back in the countries they came from. But women did come too, to the towns that

content.cn_text: 突然在金矿区兴起的城镇。她们通过开设旅馆谋生，简单的
content.en_text: suddenly sprang up by the gold fields. They made a living by starting up hotels, simple

content.cn_text: 旅馆，当然是新来者经常入住的地方。然后还有商人，他们
content.en_text: ones of course where new arrivals would often stay. And then there were businessmen, who

content.cn_text: 知道通过开设五金店等服务可以赚钱，这些店销售
content.en_text: knew there was money to be made by setting up services like hardware stores that sold

content.cn_text: 采矿和露营设备，当然还有可以存放黄金的银行。事实上，
content.en_text: mining and camping equipment, and of course banks where gold could be deposited. In fact,

content.cn_text: 商人通常比矿工做得更好。所以，正如我之前提到的，有金矿的地方，
content.en_text: the businessmen often did better than the miners. So, as I mentioned before, where there

content.cn_text: 城镇也开始发展起来。最初，建筑物是以
content.en_text: was a gold field, a town began to grow too. At first, the buildings were constructed in

content.cn_text: 临时的方式建造的，只使用帆布和木材等材料。但后来，一旦
content.en_text: a temporary fashion, just made out of materials such as canvas and timber. But later, once

content.cn_text: 补给开始抵达，就使用了混凝土，所以结构变得更加永久。但是
content.en_text: supplies started arriving, it was concrete, so the structures became more permanent. But

content.cn_text: 事实是，金矿区往往在非常偏远的地方，所以人们的生活非常艰难。
content.en_text: the fact was, the gold fields were often in very isolated areas, so life was very difficult

content.cn_text: 矿工们经常在深峡谷底部工作，所以他们必须
content.en_text: for these people. The miners were often working at the bottom of deep gorges, so they had

content.cn_text: 应对严寒气温，以及冬季冰雪融化时突发的洪水。
content.en_text: to cope with freezing temperatures, and also sudden flooding when winter ice melted in

content.cn_text: 如果这还不够糟糕，矿工们还必须面对另一个问题。
content.en_text: the mountains. If this wasn't bad enough, the miners also had to deal with another problem.

content.cn_text: 因为他们必须随身携带所有东西，所以他们的饮食非常糟糕。他们主要喝
content.en_text: Because they had to carry everything with them, they had a very poor diet. They mostly drank

content.cn_text: 茶，而他们主要吃的东西是面粉，他们以不同的方式烹饪。
content.en_text: tea, and the main thing they had to eat was flour, which they cooked in different ways.

content.cn_text: 那是当时唯一可用的。无论如何，有些矿工幸运，有些则不。但
content.en_text: That's all that was available. Anyway, some miners were lucky, others not. But the

content.cn_text: 发现的黄金无疑给奥塔哥地区带来了许多好处，也带来了一些问题。
content.en_text: gold that was found certainly brought many benefits to the Otago region, and also a couple

content.cn_text: 首先，但尼丁镇突然变成了新西兰最大的城市。
content.en_text: of problems. First of all, the town of Dunedin was suddenly transformed into the biggest city in

content.cn_text: 一些原居民抱怨土地价值上涨，
content.en_text: New Zealand. Some of the original residents complained about how the value of land was increasing,

content.cn_text: 以及卫生设施不足。你可以想象，随着数千名移民的突然涌入，
content.en_text: and about the inadequate sanitation. You can imagine how crowded it might have felt with the

content.cn_text: 可能会感到多么拥挤。但总的来说，黄金带来了许多积极的变化
content.en_text: sudden influx of thousands of immigrants. But overall, the gold brought many positive changes

content.cn_text: 给城市。航运业得到了巨大的投资和增长，正是
content.en_text: to the city. There was immense investment in growth in the shipping industry, just what the

content.cn_text: 该地区所需要的。这笔财富的另一个意外好处是，它为该国
content.en_text: region needed. Another unexpected benefit of this wealth was that it provided the funds for the

content.cn_text: 的第一所大学提供了资金。此外，随着行业的发展，对
content.en_text: first university in the country. As well as this, as the industry developed, there was a great

content.cn_text: 各类工人的需求很大，但最重要的是对经验丰富和熟练的
content.en_text: demand for workers of all sorts, but most of all there was demand for experienced and skilled

content.cn_text: 工程师的需求。他们经常创办自己的公司，其中许多公司至今仍在运营。
content.en_text: engineers. They often started their own companies, and many of these companies are still operating

content.cn_text: 我们下周将发现，这些公司仍然……
content.en_text: today. What we will discover next week is that these companies are still...

groups.desc:
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
groups.title: Questions 31-40.

items.title: Late 1850s: local governments offered a 31. ____________ to experienced gold prospectors.
May 1861: miner Gabriel Read found gold in a 32. ____________.
Businessmen offered services, e.g. hardware stores and 34. ____________.
At first, the buildings were made of canvas and timber, and later 35. ____________.
A poor diet of tea and 37. ____________.
The need for experienced workers, in particular 40. ____________.

groups.source_collect:
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
Early gold mining in the Otago region, New Zealand
Background
• 1852: there was a brief gold rush.
• Late 1850s: local governments offered a $31$ to experienced gold prospectors.
• May 1861: miner Gabriel Read found gold in a $32$.
Who took part in the gold rush?
• Mainly young single men or men leaving families at home.
• Women opened $33$ for the miners.
• Businessmen offered services, e.g. hardware stores and $34$.
What were early mining towns like?
• At first, the buildings were made of canvas and timber, and later $35$.
What difficulties did miners face at the gold fields?
• The freezing temperatures and unexpected $36$.
• A poor diet of tea and $37$.
What benefits and problems did gold bring to the Otago region?
Growth of the Otago town of Dunedin led to complaints about:
• The increasing price of $38$.
• Problems with sanitation.
The gold also resulted in:
• Investment in shipping.
• The first New Zealand $39$.
• The need for experienced workers, in particular $40$.
